Patch Set 4:

I think John's comment was not addressed.

Patch Set 4:

Ah that is easy. The function upstream used is hlist_add_behind so I wanted to test for hlist_add_after and create a wrapper since I want the code to match upstream instead of the RHEL kernels. So if we have a older kernel then
HAVE_HLIST_ADD_AFTER will be set but hlist_add_before is still nicely defined by
the macro:

#ifdef HAVE_HLIST_ADD_AFTER
#define hlist_add_behind(hnode, tail)   hlist_add_after(tail, hnode)
#endif /* HAVE_HLIST_ADD_AFTER */

Tested against upstream kernels.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

Sorry. It's good as it is.

parents.subject: LU-6652 tests: replay-dual test 18 stats wrong file

commit.subject: LU-6245 libcfs: cleanup up libcfs hash code for upstream
commit.message:
LU-6245 libcfs: cleanup up libcfs hash code for upstream

This patch brings the libcfs hash handling up to linux
kernel coding style. Various typedefs and macros have
been removed. Also support for the change of in 3.18
kernels of hlist_add_behind are also handled.

Change-Id: If50e34cfe4aaa593f755c9214f350ce21da56f20
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/14624
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: frank zago <fzago@cray.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
